android.title: Pokémon TCG Pocket - Card Game
android.summary: In Pokémon TCG Pocket, you can open two booster packs at no cost each day.
android.description:
Pokémon Trading Card Game Pocket has players in 150 countries and regions around the world.
Use your mobile devices to enjoy collecting and battling with Pokémon cards anytime, anywhere!

■ Open packs every day to collect cards!
Players can enjoy the experience of collecting, with two booster packs available to open every day at no cost. Collect different kinds of Pokémon cards, like those with nostalgic illustrations from the past, as well as completely new cards exclusive to this game!
       
■ Experience a new kind of Pokémon card!
The app features new immersive cards with illustrations that have a “3D feel.” Players can feel like they’ve leapt into the world of the card’s illustration!

■ A new way to collect with the share feature!
Sharing has just been added.
It’s a feature that lets you give one 1-4-diamond rarity card to your in-game friends—and receive one in return!

■ Trade cards with friends!
Use the trade feature to collect even more cards!
Certain cards can be traded with friends.
You can now trade cards from even the most recent booster packs. In addition, cards of 2-star rarity, Shiny 1, and Shiny 2 rarities can also be traded.
       
■ Show off your collection!
Display your cards with binders or display boards, and showcase them to players around the world! Try building a collection that you’re proud to show off!
       
■ Enjoy casual battles!
You can enjoy quick and exciting battles with your cards!
Players who want to test their skills even more can take on ranked matches.

Terms of Use: https://www.apppokemon.com/tcgp/kiyaku/kiyaku001/rule/
android.recentChanges:
● New Pulsing Aura booster packs are now available.
● Gold frames have been added to flair.
● A Claim All feature has been added for packs.
● Updates to the Obtain Items feature.
● A 2D pattern code feature has been added for friend requests.
● Revisions have been made to some features.

Overall Pocket provides a great experience for collecting virtual cards and battling other players. If the game forced players to use a variety of different decks, the ranked matches would be less predictable and way more enjoyable. Otherwise it's a great game for Pokemon fans!

The game is good, quite simple, but at the same time there is something to think about in a match - ideal for phones. But, with all due respect, the game has problems/bad experiences that shouldn't exist at all.: 1. Trades. One of the main mechanics for this kind of game. There is no chat with friends in the game, but you cannot choose the desired card from the person to whom you are trading. The most you can do is to drop your cards into the wishlist and put a mark on the "whishlist only" trade. This is real nonsense, it shouldn't be like this. Make it possible to view suitable trading cards from a friend to whom the trade itself was sent. This is exactly how it is done in any other game and in real life, no need to invent a wheel. 2. View maps. Why, when playing this game, I do not have the opportunity to view the map I am interested in in the general list (at least in gray). I understand that this is part of the "unknown" Pokemon, but this is nonsense (at least because the card art is shown in the general list of card chances and similar places in a small format, but you can't open the card completely and see the art). This is primarily a card game. The player should not access third-party resources just to view the content inside the game. 3. Animations after opening packs. Too much animation is created after opening the pack of cards, shorten at least 1/3 of this animation. After all, there is a feeling of being overloaded with unnecessary animations due to the similarity of these animations to each other. Players always want to skip them as soon as possible 4. Whishlist. Make it convenient and intuitive to manage your wish list immediately in your profile. Why the player needs to go to his cards first > select the one he needs > click the heart > go to profile > click on the whishlist > specify the selected card = this is a bad experience It should be: Profile > whishlist > choosing the right card in the search that opens = good gaming experience 5. Effect statuses on maps in battle. Why can't I click on a card under a control effect (like poison) during combat and see what the effect does? In the review, I indicated my main observations on this wonderful game. I really hope that you will see and consider my points, as I have noticed that other players have complained about this. With great respect to you and thank you!!!

This app is a great game that runs smoothly on basically all devices. The collecting aspect really heightens the experience for fans of Pokemon because you feel the excitement and joy of pulling a card that you find cool in your own specific way. The ability to show off your cards to people who are interested in the same things as you is really what dragged me into this game. Also, you get to trade cards with people who you know, and you can put those cards in binders to show off to the world. Finally, the game has a battle system that is fast paced and fun to do on the go. While being fast, it has the same tactical works that have been carried on from the real TCG. All in all, this is a great game for all ages, and I highly
recommend this!

Like many other TCG apps, such as Yu-Gi-Oh! Duel Links, this game feels heavily pay-to-win. While it’s possible to enjoy the game at first, reaching its full potential often requires spending significant amounts of money to obtain the right cards, and the pack odds are rarely in your favor.

TCG Pocket is particularly deceptive in how it handles progression. Early on, players can earn plenty of hourglasses and open packs through solo missions, creating the impression that progression is generous. However, this quickly reaches a dead end. The difficulty spike between the beginner stages and the intermediate/advanced stages is steep and feels intentionally designed to frustrate players into spending money on additional packs.

Like Duel Links, the game’s meta constantly shifts toward newly released cards. As a result, opening older packs often becomes a waste of resources, even if you personally prefer the cards they contain. The game effectively pressures players to keep chasing the latest releases in order to remain competitive.

At best, the game provides a few hours of enjoyment before players hit a paywall. Once that happens, progression slows to a crawl, and much of the fun disappears unless you’re willing to spend money.

Pokémon TCG Mobile is honestly one of the most frustrating “card games” I’ve ever played, and I say that as someone who actually enjoys the franchise.

At its core, it’s basically a slot machine with Pokémon art slapped on top. You can build a decent deck, think you’re finally getting somewhere, and then the game just says “nah” and hands you a 10-game losing streak like it’s a reward. Doesn’t matter how well you play—hand RNG decides you’re done.

You’ll open matches with zero energy, then the next game you’re flooded with nothing but energy and no Pokémon. Then there’s the coin flips that feel like they’re rigged by some ancient cursed algorithm that only exists to watch you suffer. Heads when it helps the opponent, tails when it matters for you. Every time.

What makes it worse is the illusion that skill matters. It feels like you’re making decisions, but half the time the outcome is already decided before you even draw your first card. You’re just there for the ride while the game decides whether you’re allowed to win today or not.

And the matchmaking? Somehow always perfectly tuned to pair you with decks that counter yours in the most annoying way possible, like it’s personal.

Honestly, it’s a fun concept ruined by pure, unfiltered RNG hell. If you enjoy being emotionally tested by a digital coin flip simulator, then yeah, it’s great. Otherwise, prepare for the game to bless you with the sacred 10-loss streak experience over and over again.

At first the game was really fun and I love the artwork that I could collect. In the last three months straight: the pull rates have gotten significantly worse, wonderpicks are hardly even worth doing and I get the card I want WAY less than the 1/5 ratio it should be at, and completing sets have been practically impossible unless you spend a whole week trading. It's become repetitively disappointing and bland, you'll hardly pull a decent card for a month unless you spend money nowadays.

It's decent if you like the idea of card collecting but don't want to spend money. But they really want you to spend money. You can open a few packs a day, it's kind of boring once you finish all the solo matches (best way to get more packs). Trading is helpful, but (like many other aspects) it isn't balanced well. You can only trade with one person at a time, so you're sitting waiting for a response for 2 days unable to do any other trades. Online battles are pointless. App is also very laggy.

Love Pokémon, but this app is getting more frustrating unfortunately. Bigger sets with one pack make them hard to complete, and it's become hard to use the new decks before they get power crept. Trading partners are also difficult to find. Art is nice and I do mostly like battling it's just personally harder to keep interest nowadays (ESPECIALLY as free-to-play). Also wish there were more game modes / events for non-meta cards to be used more. Hoping for positive changes :)

The "rewards" system in this game is abysmal. If you attain a certain rank from the last season and weren't able to play ranked (or didnt want to) and didn't play a current ranked game in the most recent season you obtain no rewards whatsoever moving forward. There is no notification or warning for that. There are also no notifications when ranked will end for a particular season. if you enjoy 'gatcha" games with poor pull rates, slow UI, and little-to-no consumer consideration; download now.

Pretty fun, and MUCH simpler than the paper cards version. However, this game always needs to update, and when it does that, it takes a very long time. Also, it's always the worst possible times when it must update. On a plane with no WiFi? Game requires update. Overall though, pretty good.

It's alright. It gives you that pack-ripping and card-collecting feeling, even just being digital. The art design on a lot of cards is cool. You can enjoy it without spending money, but of course, you'll be much more limited. Their desire is for you to spend money. It's Pokémon, of course they want your money. The battle system is ok. There's obvious skill in deck building, but it really comes down to the luck of the draws and coin flips during play. Which can feel rigged at times.
